design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New flagship topic: proximal difference-in-differences bridge frontier with negative controls. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Miao-Geng-Tchetgen proximal causal inference, Tchetgen-Tchetgen negative-control bridge work, standard two-period and staggered DiD identification, and recent papers on difference-in-differences with negative controls. Our theorem is not those because the focal object is a finite pretrend-proxy bridge operator that sharply separates three regimes: ordinary parallel-trends DiD identifies ATT, proximal negative-control DiD identifies ATT even when parallel trends fails, and neither identifies ATT. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an explicit 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy witness with observed negative-control outcome and exposure, a bridge-rank condition computed from pre-period moments, and a non-equivalence theorem showing the proximal bridge and parallel-trends restrictions are neither nested nor equivalent. If the proposal reduces to generic proximal completeness, ordinary DiD placebo tests, support coverage, or a definition-unfold rank condition without a worked witness and non-equivalence class, pivot or stop early.

proposed_from.topic: New flagship topic: proximal difference-in-differences bridge frontier with negative controls. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchors are Miao-Geng-Tchetgen proximal causal inference, Tchetgen-Tchetgen negative-control bridge work, standard two-period and staggered DiD identification, and recent papers on difference-in-differences with negative controls. Our theorem is not those because the focal object is a finite pretrend-proxy bridge operator that sharply separates three regimes: ordinary parallel-trends DiD identifies ATT, proximal negative-control DiD identifies ATT even when parallel trends fails, and neither identifies ATT. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an explicit 2-group x 3-period x binary-proxy witness with observed negative-control outcome and exposure, a bridge-rank condition computed from pre-period moments, and a non-equivalence theorem showing the proximal bridge and parallel-trends restrictions are neither nested nor equivalent. If the proposal reduces to generic proximal completeness, ordinary DiD placebo tests, support coverage, or a definition-unfold rank condition without a worked witness and non-equivalence class, pivot or stop early.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue prior art is the downgraded eid_proximal_phase_v1 bank, especially the reviewer request for finite examples exhibiting all three proximal phases with explicit endpoint certificates. Version 2 keeps the DiD-specific bridge frontier and now supplies finite observed and latent laws rather than a generic bridge-envelope LP.","published_axis":"The closest published papers give generic proxy rank identification, negative-control readings of DiD, direct NC-DiD estimators, and calibration designs. The revised gap is the finite preperiod-proxy bridge frontier for ATT: a computable binary table condition separating ordinary DiD, proximal DiD, and nonidentification."}
seed_list: Characterize the finite binary-proxy bridge-rank ATT frontier for two-group three-period negative-control DiD and exhibit all three regimes.
Construct a non-equivalence theorem separating additive parallel trends, odds-ratio equi-confounding, single-proxy calibration, and double-proxy bridge restrictions.
Derive sharp ATT bounds when the binary-proxy bridge rank fails but preperiod negative-control moments still restrict trend bias.
Define a native estimator-equivalence threshold between proximal OR/IPW/DR bridge objectives and minimax negative-control bridge objectives.
Turn multiple pre-treatment periods into a finite proximal bridge operator and compare it to double-DID and K-DID moments.
Give an open-class pairwise non-nesting witness for negative-control DiD assumption systems using finite observable laws.
Build a critical-delta sensitivity frontier for proximal DiD when bridge stability is locally violated.
Identify a joint family of ATT, ATU, and trend-bias functionals from one finite negative-control bridge object.
Construct a sharp finite fallback interval for proximal DiD under rank-deficient proxy matrices with endpoint latent completions.
Develop a regularized finite bridge estimator whose rate changes at the bridge-rank frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the finite binary-proxy bridge-rank ATT frontier for two-group three-period negative-control DiD and exhibit all three regimes.

anchor_paper.author: Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen; Sofer et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Proxy rank identifies effects; DiD can be read as a negative-outcome-control strategy.
anchor_paper.location: Miao et al. Section 3.2; Sofer et al. Section 3

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends proximal rank to DiD, but the nontrivial part is the three-regime finite witness and null-loading frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MiaoGengTchetgen2018ProxyVariables because finite rank already gives identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: Miao et al. do not compare against ordinary parallel trends or prove the finite DiD nonidentification branch.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not generic proximal completeness because the focal object is the observed (B_P,b_P,pi_P) DiD frontier with a computed witness.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the bridge-rank condition is paired with a non-equivalence class and explicit 2x3 binary-proxy exhibit.

adversarial_referee.instance: Rank frontier could read as generic completeness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal exhibits the finite DiD bridge operator and all three regimes in Section 9.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity may be left as informal.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 1 explicitly labels necessity open and routes it through equal-P latent completions.

adversarial_referee.instance: Binary witness could sit on a boundary.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: All displayed probabilities are interior and determinant/range checks are explicit.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a non-equivalence theorem separating additive parallel trends, odds-ratio equi-confounding, single-proxy calibration, and double-proxy bridge restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson; Park, Richardson, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Universal DiD replaces additive parallel trends; single-proxy ATT differs from two-proxy proximal identification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample seed is stronger because it targets named published restrictions and finite observable laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a scale-change comparison among known DiD assumptions.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed demands pairwise observed-law non-nesting and ATT divergence, not verbal comparison.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not TchetgenParkRichardson2024UniversalDID because it compares four restriction classes on the same finite ATT law.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: produces a witness class, not a relabelled assumption list.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The target is deliberately finite-panel ATT; family extension is secondary.
Must be promoted to open finite classes to stay flagship.

adversarial_referee.instance: Universal DiD may not claim nesting.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed targets literature tension, not a false claim of nesting.

seed_details.one_line: Derive sharp ATT bounds when the binary-proxy bridge rank fails but preperiod negative-control moments still restrict trend bias.

anchor_paper.author: Penning de Vries and Groenwold; Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen; Rambachan and Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Rank failure need not imply useless preperiod information if weakened restrictions still bound trend bias.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens rank, but stronger if endpoints are sharp latent completions rather than generic LP bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from finite LP partial identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: The missing part is the sharp causal realization of rank-deficient negative-control DiD endpoints.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not RambachanRoth2023ParallelTrends because the restriction is proxy-moment rank failure, not a smoothness budget.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless endpoint realization is proved.

seed_details.one_line: Define a native estimator-equivalence threshold between proximal OR/IPW/DR bridge objectives and minimax negative-control bridge objectives.

anchor_paper.author: Prior proposal reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewer asked for a native estimator-frontier theorem with computable threshold.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: a computable threshold that sharply separates asymptotic equivalence and strict non-equivalence

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Equivalence seed is strong only if native published estimator objectives are pinned exactly.
seed_details.referee_objection: Prior review says this collapses to generic finite convex algebra.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed would avoid shared exact-fiber reduction and define each native objective separately.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the rejected eid_proximal_phase angle because it forbids a shared exact-fiber selector.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with named native losses; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: Named estimators may not match finite objectives.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must quote objective definitions before pursuing.
Needs strict estimator divergence, not interval comparison.

adversarial_referee.instance: Published minimax papers may use different targets.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Reviewer already flagged this; seed is secondary.

seed_details.one_line: Turn multiple pre-treatment periods into a finite proximal bridge operator and compare it to double-DID and K-DID moments.

anchor_paper.author: Egami and Yamauchi; Imbens, Kallus, and Mao
anchor_paper.claim: Multiple preperiods support trend tests and bridge functions, but not a minimal negative-control bridge table.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: multiple pretreatment periods can assess parallel trends

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=2 to T>2 extension is weak unless a rank boundary appears.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just K-DID with proxy notation.
seed_details.rebuttal: A valid version must prove a bridge-rank iff not implied by K-DID moments.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not EgamiYamauchi2023MultiplePretreatmentDID because it uses proxy bridge rank rather than trend restrictions.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless the bridge operator is independently evaluable.

seed_details.one_line: Give an open-class pairwise non-nesting witness for negative-control DiD assumption systems using finite observable laws.

anchor_paper.author: Penning de Vries and Groenwold
anchor_paper.claim: Different negative-control and DiD approaches rely on distinct strong assumptions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: each relies on distinct strong assumptions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample search is stronger if promoted from single table to open class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an assumption taxonomy, not a theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The finite observed-law non-nesting table with ATT divergence is theorem-level.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not PenningDeVriesGroenwold2023NegativeControls because it supplies finite non-nesting laws.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if open neighborhoods are stated.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: This seed is weaker than the chosen bridge-frontier seed.

seed_details.one_line: Build a critical-delta sensitivity frontier for proximal DiD when bridge stability is locally violated.

anchor_paper.author: Rambachan and Roth
anchor_paper.claim: Robust DiD inference restricts post-treatment violations using pretrend differences.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: restrictions on how post-treatment violations can differ from observed pretrend differences

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity seed needs explicit L(delta), U(delta), and critical delta to avoid sufficient-only sensitivity.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is Rambachan-Roth with negative controls added.
seed_details.rebuttal: A real version would define delta in bridge-moment space, not trend-smoothness space.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not RambachanRoth2023ParallelTrends because the violation metric is proxy-bridge residual.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier until closed-form critical delta is computed.

seed_details.one_line: Identify a joint family of ATT, ATU, and trend-bias functionals from one finite negative-control bridge object.

anchor_paper.author: Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen; Cui et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Bridge functions can identify treatment-effect functionals under proxy restrictions.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: proximal ATE and ATT efficiency theory

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Family seed is stronger if one bridge primitive identifies several named estimands jointly.
seed_details.referee_objection: ATT, ATU, and trend bias are routine once h is known.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nonroutine part would be joint null-loading invariance when h is not unique.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024JASA because it targets finite DiD joint null-loading conditions.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier without a nonunique-bridge theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a sharp finite fallback interval for proximal DiD under rank-deficient proxy matrices with endpoint latent completions.

anchor_paper.author: Prior proposal reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer asked for finite examples with endpoint certificates when bridge rank is deficient.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Add finite examples exhibiting all three phases with explicit endpoint LP certificates

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: LP fallback is weak unless endpoint latent completions make bounds sharp.
seed_details.referee_objection: This repeats rejected eid_proximal_phase finite LP interval work.
seed_details.rebuttal: It must specialize to DiD negative-control moments and provide endpoint completions absent from the rejected angle.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the rejected proximal-phase LP because the observed design is two-group three-period DiD.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless endpoint certificates are explicit.

seed_details.one_line: Develop a regularized finite bridge estimator whose rate changes at the bridge-rank frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Gregori; Cui et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Negative-control DiD estimators and proximal DR estimators exist, but no finite rank-margin rate frontier is stated.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: does not state a finite preperiod binary-proxy rank frontier

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator-duality seed is strong only if the rate changes at the rank margin.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard regularized inverse-problem asymptotics.
seed_details.rebuttal: The DiD-specific novelty would need a finite pretrend rank-margin object tied to ATT correction.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024JASA because it targets the finite DiD rank frontier.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier until the rate theorem is specified.

proposed_from.literature_map: 1. Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika) identify effects with two proxies and rank conditions. 2. Sofer et al. (2016, Statistical Science) interpret ordinary DiD as negative-outcome-control identification under equal unobserved-confounder associations. 3. Tchetgen Tchetgen et al. (2024, Statistical Science) give the broader proximal bridge framework. 4. Cui et al. (2024, JASA) develop proximal ATT/ATE efficiency under bridge and completeness conditions. 5. Penning de Vries and Groenwold (2023, SMMR) compare COCA, DiD, generalized DiD, and double-negative-control designs and emphasize assumption fragility. 6. Gregori (2025, EMAC) gives an explicit negative-control DiD estimator but not a finite bridge-rank frontier. 7. Zhang et al. (2025, npj Digital Medicine) use negative-control-calibrated DiD diagnostics. 8. Park, Richardson, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, Biometrics) separate single-proxy ATT identification from two-proxy proximal identification. 9. Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson (2024, Epidemiology) replace additive parallel trends with odds-ratio equi-confounding. 10. Rambachan and Roth (2023, ReStud) bound DiD violations using pretrend restrictions, not proxy bridge rank. 11. Egami and Yamauchi (2023, Political Analysis) use multiple pretreatment periods for testing and weaker trend assumptions. 12. Imbens, Kallus, and Mao (2021, arXiv) use minimal bridge functions in panel factor settings. 13. Liu, Tchetgen Tchetgen, and Varjao (2024, AISTATS) bring proximal reasoning to synthetic controls. 14. The live gap is the finite bridge frontier separating parallel-trends-only, proximal-only, and neither-identified regimes.

banked_reason: Stopped after two D-0.5 REVISE@field reviews under flagship target: the finite bridge-rank frontier remained a proximal null-annihilator/bridge-fiber definition specialized to DiD, with no named NC-DiD estimator frontier or strict extension beyond existing proximal panel bridge results.
